P2: Mexican Mint (Black Pot | Soil | Sen.details: A young plant with multiple leaves is clearly visible in the black pot, along with soil and sensor modules, matching the registry.

Unmonitored: Money Plant (White Cup | Wa.details: A single-leaf plant cutting is visible in water within the white cup, consistent with water propagation and the registry.

EARLIEST.leaf_turgor_posture: Leaves appear turgid, slightly cupped upwards, indicating good hydration.
EARLIEST.soil_condition: Dark brown, surface appears somewhat dry but not severely cracked. Some small debris present.
EARLIEST.sensor_status: White sensor module partially inserted into soil, purple PCB sensor on rim, both present and connected.

T-2.leaf_color: Slightly darker green compared to EARLIEST.
T-2.leaf_turgor_posture: Leaves appear slightly less turgid, with some lower leaves showing a subtle downward droop. Still generally healthy.
T-2.soil_condition: Appears slightly darker and possibly moister on the surface, suggesting a recent watering event.

T-1.leaf_color: Returns to a lighter green, similar to EARLIEST.
T-1.leaf_turgor_posture: Leaves appear more spread out and turgid again, indicating improved hydration or recovery.
T-1.soil_condition: Surface appears a bit drier again, with some lighter soil particles visible, suggesting evaporation.

CURRENT.leaf_color: Light to medium green, consistent with T-1.
CURRENT.leaf_turgor_posture: Leaves maintain good turgor and posture, consistent with T-1.
CURRENT.soil_condition: Consistent with T-1, somewhat dry on the surface.

Black_Pot_Soil.texture_changes: Fluctuates between somewhat dry (EARLIEST, T-1, CURRENT) and slightly moister (T-2). No severe cracking observed.
Black_Pot_Soil.incidental_growth: No weeds, moss, or secondary seedlings detected.

White_Cup_Water.clarity_level: Consistently clear water, stable level across all images. No algae or cloudiness.
White_Cup_Water.debris: No debris observed in the water.

Desk_Surface.debris_anomalies: Consistently clean and dark surface. No debris, spills, or other anomalies detected.

temporal_deltas.P2_Mexican_Mint: The Mexican Mint shows minor, cyclical changes. A slight darkening of leaves and potential decrease in turgor at T-2 suggests a response to watering, followed by recovery to a lighter green and turgid state as the soil surface dries. No significant growth or decline.
temporal_deltas.Unmonitored_Money_Plant: The Money Plant cutting in water propagation shows remarkable stability across all images, with no discernible changes in leaf color, turgor, or water level/clarity.

visual_health_inference.P2_Mexican_Mint: The plant appears generally healthy. The observed fluctuations in leaf color and turgor, coupled with changes in soil moisture, are consistent with normal physiological responses to watering cycles. There are no visual indicators of severe stress, disease, or nutrient deficiencies.
visual_health_inference.Unmonitored_Money_Plant: The Money Plant cutting appears to be in excellent health. Its single leaf maintains consistent dark green color and turgor, indicating successful water uptake and stability in its propagation environment.

vision_report.narrative_description: The chronological audit of the indoor plant biome reveals a stable and generally healthy environment. The P2 Mexican Mint in the black pot exhibits expected minor fluctuations in leaf turgor and color, correlating with observed changes in soil moisture, suggesting a healthy response to its watering regimen. No signs of distress or significant growth/decline are noted. Concurrently, the unmonitored Money Plant in the white cup, undergoing water propagation, demonstrates remarkable consistency across all images, maintaining a vibrant, turgid leaf. No systemic losses, new introductions, incidental growth, or biome anomalies were detected throughout the observation period. Both specimens appear to be thriving under the current conditions.
